description:
You are a release-manager who needs to hand off a concise error report to the ops team before tonight’s deployment.

    Context already present on the system:
    • The directory /home/user/build-logs/ exists.  
    • Inside it there is a file named release_2023-05-18.log that contains a mixed stream of INFO, WARN and ERROR lines for several build-pipeline modules (compile, test, deploy, etc.).  

    Your goal
    1. Create a new file called /home/user/build-logs/release_2023-05-18-deploy-errors.log.
    2. This new file must contain **only** the lines that simultaneously meet BOTH of the following conditions:  
       a) The log-level field is exactly “[ERROR]”.  
       b) The module field is exactly “[deploy]”.  
       (Both fields are surrounded by square brackets and appear exactly once in every log line.)
    3. Preserve the original chronological order of the matching lines; do not add, reorder, or delete characters within a line.
    4. Use a single regex-based filtering command (e.g., grep, sed, awk with a regular expression).  
       • You may use additional redirection or pipes, but the filtering logic must be expressed in one command-line invocation.
    5. Do not modify release_2023-05-18.log. Only read from it.
    6. No other files should be created or modified.

    Output format the automated test will verify
    • /home/user/build-logs/release_2023-05-18-deploy-errors.log must exist.  
    • It must contain exactly 3 lines (newline-terminated) whose verbatim text is:  
      [2023-05-18 14:22:20] [ERROR] [deploy] Cannot connect to production server  
      [2023-05-18 14:22:30] [ERROR] [deploy] Deployment aborted due to previous errors  
      [2023-05-18 14:22:45] [ERROR] [deploy] Retry failed, manual intervention required  

    (A trailing newline after the third line is required; no extra blank lines before or after.)

    Once you have created the file, you’re done—no additional console output is necessary, but feel free to display the resulting file’s contents to confirm.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
    Path: /home/user/build-logs/release_2023-05-18.log  
    Exact contents (each line terminated with “\n”):

    [2023-05-18 14:22:01] [INFO]  [compile] Compilation successful
    [2023-05-18 14:22:05] [WARN]  [test]    Unit test took longer than expected
    [2023-05-18 14:22:10] [ERROR] [compile] Missing semicolon in source/foo.c
    [2023-05-18 14:22:15] [INFO]  [package] Packaging artifacts
    [2023-05-18 14:22:20] [ERROR] [deploy]  Cannot connect to production server
    [2023-05-18 14:22:25] [INFO]  [deploy]  Connection re-established
    [2023-05-18 14:22:30] [ERROR] [deploy]  Deployment aborted due to previous errors
    [2023-05-18 14:22:35] [INFO]  [cleanup] Temporary files removed
    [2023-05-18 14:22:40] [WARN]  [deploy]  Retrying deployment
    [2023-05-18 14:22:45] [ERROR] [deploy]  Retry failed, manual intervention required

    (All lines use single spaces exactly as shown; note that the WARN and INFO lines for deploy must not be included in the filtered file.)

    Expected file created by the agent:
    Path: /home/user/build-logs/release_2023-05-18-deploy-errors.log  
    Expected contents (each line terminated with “\n”):

    [2023-05-18 14:22:20] [ERROR] [deploy]  Cannot connect to production server
    [2023-05-18 14:22:30] [ERROR] [deploy]  Deployment aborted due to previous errors
    [2023-05-18 14:22:45] [ERROR] [deploy]  Retry failed, manual intervention required
